Fundación Escaramuza
Donate

Escaramuza Charra · Therapeutic Riding

Riding for heritage.
Riding to heal.

An escaramuza team and foundation. The horses that carry our tradition also carry children who need them most — through therapeutic riding sessions built on trust, patience, and discipline.

Donate See our work

What we are

Three pillars, one mission.

A women's equestrian tradition that's also a vehicle for healing — funded by the community it serves.

01

Heritage

An all-women's tradition since the Revolution.

Escaramuza charra is the only women's equestrian sport in Mexican charrería. Eight riders, side-saddle, in choreographed maneuvers — a living tradition carried since the women of the Mexican Revolution.

02

Healing

The same horses. A different rider.

The horses that perform also serve children with autism, physical injuries, and developmental needs through therapeutic riding sessions guided by trained staff and trained sidewalkers.

03

Support

Performances and donations keep a child in the saddle.

Bookings, sponsorships, and individual gifts fund the horses, the equipment, and the lessons. Every contribution goes directly to keeping the program running.

Our story

From the lienzo to the lesson.

The same care that goes into a routine — the trust between rider and horse, the discipline, the patience — is what makes our therapy program work.

This team rides in competition. It also rides for children who otherwise wouldn't get the chance. The connection between an escaramuza and her horse — built over years of practice and presence — is the same connection that helps a child with autism find calm, a child recovering from injury find balance, a child who's been told what they can't do find what they can.

Therapeutic Riding

How a session works.

A structured, safety-first program for children with autism, physical injuries, and developmental needs. Designed around the rhythm of the horse — and the child who's riding.

Step 01

Inquiry & evaluation

A short intake call to understand the rider's needs, history, and goals.

Step 02

Physician's statement

A simple form from the rider's doctor confirms they're cleared to participate.

Step 03

The lesson

Each rider is paired with a trained horse and supported by an instructor plus trained sidewalkers.

Step 04

Progress over time

Sessions build core strength, balance, focus, communication, and confidence — week by week.

ASTM/SEI helmets required Trained sidewalkers PATH Intl.–aligned practices Physician sign-off required

Performances

Book the team.

Eight riders in full traje charro, choreographed at speed to live or recorded music. Festivals, fundraisers, weddings, community events.

Every booking directly funds the therapeutic riding program. The team travels regionally; ask about your venue and date.

Book a performance
▶

Performance reel

(Drops in once final video is provided)

The team

Eight riders. One family.

Sisters, cousins, friends, daughters of the women who rode before them. Off the horse, they're students, workers, mothers. On the horse, they're a single unit — eight bodies moving as one.

Individual rider and horse bios coming soon — including the four horses that share their time between the arena and the therapy program.

Get involved

Three ways to keep the program riding.

Donate

One-time or monthly.

$50 covers a single therapy session. $250 supports a full month for one rider. Recurring gifts make the most difference.

$25 $50 $100 $250
Make a gift
Sponsor

A horse. A child's season. A performance.

Annual sponsorships cover a specific horse's feed and vet care, a child's full season of lessons, or a performance — with recognition for your business or family.

Become a sponsor
Volunteer

Sidewalkers, barn help, event days.

No horse experience required for most roles. We train sidewalkers — the volunteers who walk alongside the horse during a therapy lesson.

Sign up
